title: patagonium mucronatum
side_effects: Patagonium mucronatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting the plant's toxic compounds can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while topical application can cause redness, itching, and inflammation. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils can also cause respiratory issues, including shortness of breath and coughing, and may exacerbate conditions like asthma. In severe cases, improper use of Patagonium mucronatum can lead to anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction.
